ew_context: Price finding support at Fibonacci 61.8% retracement level - typical wave 2 or corrective B wave reversal zone
pattern_desc: Bounce developing from 4081.46 Fibonacci support with RSI neutrally positioned at 51.0%. Key resistance at 4138.50 swing high.

market_context: Gold steadying after six-month lows with focus on inflation data, while Iran tensions provide safe-haven underpinning
full_analysis:
## Market Structure & Technical Context

Price is testing critical Fibonacci 61.8% retracement support at 4081.46 following the decline from 4245.50 swing high to 4046.20 swing low. This level represents classic wave 2 or corrective B wave territory where reversals frequently develop. Gold currently trades at entry level with both EMA20 (4130.25) and EMA50 (4193.20) remaining bearishly aligned above price, creating overhead resistance.

RSI at 51.0% shows neutral momentum with room for upside expansion, while the elevated ATR of 26.90 reflects the recent volatility. Turbulent flow dynamics with misaligned momentum suggest choppy conditions but support the potential for impulsive moves.

## Key Levels

- **Resistance**: 4138.50 (recent swing high and primary target area), 4163.60 (measured move target)
- **Support**: 4081.46 (current Fibonacci support), 4046.20 (structural swing low)

## Invalidation Level

Close below 4046.20 on H4 timeframe would break below the corrective low and negate the Fibonacci bounce structure.

## Macro & Flow Context

Gold has steadied after hitting six-month lows with markets focused on upcoming inflation data. US-Iran tensions provide safe-haven support, while the turbulent CFD flow pattern suggests institutional repositioning at these technical levels.
